stories.title: Europe’s Socialists face far-right reckoning after Romania deal
stories.summary: STRASBOURG — Europe’s Socialists have spent years scolding the center-right for cutting deals with the far right. Now they are facing the same charge. When Romania’s Social Democrats (PSD) on Monday teamed up with the far-right Alliance for the Union of Romanians (AUR) to topple Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan and the centrist coalition government they had previously backed, lawmakers in Brussels said the center left is doing what exactly what it has long accused the center-right European People&#82

stories.title: 5 ways the Iran war shows NATO is not ready to fight Russia
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — NATO has stayed out of the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran, but the conflict has nevertheless exposed cracks in the alliance’s defenses that would see it struggle if Russia attacks. “The wars in Ukraine and the Middle East are not separate phenomena; there is much to learn from both in thinking about the wars of tomorrow,” Gen. Dominique Tardif, France’s deputy air force chief, said. “These combined lessons should lead us to a better understanding of how to direct capability development.” Eu

stories.title: Odesa bears brunt of latest Russian attacks on Ukraine – as it happened
stories.summary: Across country, at least 14 have been injured as Zelenskyy highlights importance of air defences Top EU officials and Hungary’s incoming government will discuss on Wednesday the changes Budapest needs to push through to release €17bn in EU funds that have been blocked due to rule-of-law concerns under the outgoing government of Viktor Orbán. stories.title: Un tanneur de LVMH, lié à la déforestation, se démène contre la réglementation européenne
stories.summary: BRUXELLES — Nuti Ivo, une tannerie italienne appartenant au groupe LVMH, a acheté des peaux animales à des entreprises paraguayennes liées à la déforestation, selon l’enquête de l’ONG Global Witness, alors même que son dirigeant tente d’obtenir des exemptions dans le cadre de la réglementation européenne contre la déforestation. Avec le soutien de députés et de responsables publics européens, l’industrie du cuir fait campagne auprès de la Commission européenne pour que ses nouvelles règles sur l

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Arrests over Russian GRU-linked murder plots in Lithuania
stories.summary: Fundraiser for Ukraine was among targets, Lithuanian authorities allege; Kyiv collars Israel over Russian shipments of stolen Ukrainian grain. What we know on day 1,525 Continue reading...

stories.title: Orbán may be gone, but his prejudices are now baked into the European political mainstream | Shada Islam
stories.summary: EU leaders have normalised once fringe racist narratives in their migration, border control and even foreign policies For years, Viktor Orbán, with his anti-migrant and white Christian nationalist rhetoric – sentiments that endeared him to Donald Trump and his Maga base – offered his European counterparts the comforting fiction that racism in the EU was the preserve of a few unsavoury men and women. Unfortunately, it isn’t that simple. Racism is not the work of one individual. It is structural.

stories.title: Iványi Gábor nem ambicionálja a köztársasági elnöki pozíciót
stories.summary: Azt mondta, nem hiányzik az élettörténetéből, és nem akar megküzdeni érte.

stories.title: Miből fog élni Orbán Viktor?
stories.summary: A leköszönő miniszterelnök elveszíti mindkét jól fizető állását, de búcsúpénzként kiutalnak még neki bruttó 38,8 milliót. A törvény szerint évekig jár neki a hivatali autó sofőrrel és a titkárság irodával. A vagyonnyilatkozatait sokszor szinte üresen adta le, papíron alig tudott valamit félretenni 36 év alatt. Mostantól nem kell színt vallania, de a mentelmi joga is megszűnik.

stories.title: Egy férfi beismerte, hogy részt vett a Run-DMC tagjának, Jam Master Jaynek meggyilkolásában
stories.summary: Tudta, hogy le fogják lőni a zenészt, és beengedte a gyilkosokat a stúdióba.

stories.title: A Fidesz-frakció munkatársának lakásában lakik a Magyarországra menekült körözött lengyel politikus
stories.summary: A Direkt36 munkatársai egy budai lakásban találták meg a hazájában korrupcióval vádolt Marcin Romanowskit. Az ingatlan egyik tulajdonosát tavaly ősszel szerződtette le a Kocsis Máté által vezetett fideszes frakció titkári feladatokra.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: A Tisza nem szavazza meg az Országgyűlés alelnökének Szijjártó Pétert
stories.summary: A megválasztott miniszterelnök a volt külügyminisztert a magyar érdekek elárulójának nevezte.

stories.title: Hankó Balázs szerint nem igaz, hogy a Fidesz-kampány celebjei kapták meg az NKA-s milliárdokat
stories.summary: A kulturális miniszter azt mondta, a pályázatok a magyar kultúra egészét szolgálják, és a forrásokat szakmai szempontok alapján osztották ki.

stories.title: Edith Eva Eger, writer, psychologist, and Holocaust survivor dies
stories.summary: The clinical psychologist of Hungarian descent, best known for her book, "The Choice", was 98 years old.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor azon még nem gondolkozott, miből fog megélni
stories.summary: Kedden tartja a Fidesz a választmányi ülését, ahol dönthetnek a párt tisztújító kongresszusának összehívásáról. Az ülésre érkező Orbán Balázs, Tuzson Bence, Vitályos Eszter nem nyilatkozott a sajtónak. Kubatov Gábor azt mondta, mindenkinek megvan a személyes felelőssége a választási vereségben, neki is, Orbánnak is. Deutsch Tamás szerint az a fideszes, aki azt mondja a nyilvánosság előtt, hogy nem hibázott, az téved.

stories.title: Vitályos Esztert jelöli a Fidesz az Országgyűlés alelnökének

stories.summary: Manfred Weber szerint az eredmény azt mutatja, hogy a populisták nem szállítanak eredményeket. Eltért a véleménye a zöldek társelnökétől abban, hogyan kezeljék a magyar hetes cikkes eljárást.

stories.title: Gyerekként még elbújt, nehogy versenyeznie kelljen, most övé lett a maraton korszakos világrekordja
stories.summary: A kenyai Sabastian Sawe néhány éve még teljesen ismeretlen volt, most pedig első futóként teljesítette két órán belül a maratont. Rekordja előtt két szelet kenyeret és mézet dobott be reggelire. Arra kérte a doppingellenőröket, hogy a lehető legtöbbször teszteljék.

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: Europe is in a profound state of crisis. Luckily, we know what to do | Nathalie Tocci and Anu Bradford
stories.summary: We assembled a group of the continent’s leading thinkers to assess the threats: their warnings are stark, but the remedy is within reach Caught between Vladimir Putin’s Russia, Donald Trump’s US and Xi Jinping’s China, Europe appears in a state of profound crisis, the narrative about its future often filled with fatalism. stories.title: Altman apologizes after OpenAI failed to alert police before fatal Canada shooting
stories.summary: OpenAI said the company had identified an account using abuse-detection efforts, but determined at the time it didn’t meet threshold for legal referral The head of OpenAI has written a letter apologizing that his company didn’t alert law enforcement about the online behavior of a person who shot and killed eight people in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia. In the letter posted on Friday, Sam Altman expressed his deepest condolences to the entire community. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: King Charles to argue for strong US-UK partnership in address to Congress
stories.summary: King Charles will use his speech to Congress to help repair the “special relationship” between the U.S. and Britain that has been under strain over the Iran war. The king plans to focus on reconciliation and renewal in a speech Tuesday before the House and Senate that is expected to run about 20 minutes, according to royal aides. stories.title: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report
stories.summary: Regime used its isolation after closing borders to escalate killings when global scrutiny disappeared, NGO claims North Korea dramatically increased its use of the death penalty after closing its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, using its isolation to escalate killings when international scrutiny disappeared, according to a report mapping 13 years of executions under the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un . Blair unexpectedly went into labor shortly before her full Delta flight from Atlanta to Portland touched down on Friday night. stories.title: Humanoid robots to become baggage handlers in Japan airport experiment
stories.summary: Japan Airlines will introduce the robots for trial run at a Tokyo airport amid country’s surge in inbound tourism and worsening labour shortages Japan’s famously conscientious but overburdened baggage handlers will soon be joined by extra staff at Tokyo’s Haneda airport – although their new colleagues will need to take regular recharging breaks. stories.summary: Education secretary says children will face restrictions and government will consider range of views on their form Children in the UK will face restrictions on their use of social media but the government remains open-minded about what form the limits will take, Bridget Phillipson, the education secretary, has said. stories.title: British politicians are doing God. Voters would rather they didn’t.
stories.summary: LONDON — The British public want politicians to steer clear of religion just as key Westminster figures amp up debates on Christianity’s role in the country, new polling shows. A survey for POLITICO by independent London-based polling company Public First found 65 percent of voters back a separation between Christianity and politics — a view held by a clear majority of supporters of all the main political parties. stories.title: Malta to hold snap elections in May
stories.summary: Malta’s Prime Minister Robert Abela on Monday evening called a snap general election, citing a need for “stability” amid geopolitical turmoil. The vote will take place on May 30, nine months before the end of the prime minister’s term. “In light of the current global realities and challenges ahead, our country needs a renewed mandate to move forward with stability. I will be recommending to H.E. President Myriam Spiteri Debono the dissolution of Parliament for a gen

stories.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors
stories.summary: Efforts continue to free two trapped passengers in wreckage after long-distance train collides with commuter train outside Jakarta, injuring 81 The death toll from a train collision near the Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 14 with another 84 injured, the train operator said on Tuesday, as rescuers worked to extract survivors still trapped in the wreckage. stories.title: Shadow minister says Australia ‘ill-prepared’ for conflict – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The White House has nominated David Brat , a former Republican member of the US House of Representatives, to be the next ambassador to Australia . Brat represented Virginia in Congress and served two terms before he was defeated by a Democrat in 2018 in a close race. He is currently a vice-president of business relations at Virginia’s Liberty University. It wouldn’t fund the entire amount of that extra storage,

stories.title: ‘Protected for another century’: experts lift 15-tonne foremast from HMS Victory
stories.summary: In latest stage of £42m restoration project, 30-strong team removed mast from Nelson’s Battle of Trafalgar flagship There is only one correct way to extricate a 15-tonne wrought iron mast from one of the world’s most famous and beloved warships – very slowly, and with extreme care. Which is precisely what a 30-strong team, led by shipwrights and riggers, did on Monday night into Tuesday morning, when they lifted the foremast from HMS Victory as part of a £42m conservation project. Continue readi

stories.title: Author of novel depicting toddler role-play spared jail after being convicted of writing child abuse material
stories.summary: Lauren Ashley Mastrosa given 18-month community corrections order after book was read by handful of advance readers The author behind an offensive novel depicting toddler role-play has been convicted but spared jail for penning child abuse material. Lauren Ashley Mastrosa, a 34-year-old former marketing executive for a Christian charity, wrote Daddy’s Little Toy under the pen name Tori Woods and published it through an online pre-release in March 2025. Continue reading...

stories.title: Former top civil servant tells MPs he was not consulted about Mandelson appointment, but suggests he should have been - UK politics live
stories.summary: Philip Barton gives evidence to foreign affairs committee, with Morgan McSweeney due later this morning, ahead of vote this afternoon Q: Do you think your concerns about Mandelson had any influence on David Lammy asking you to leave early? No, says Barton. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mexico warns US involvement in anti-drug operation should not be repeated
stories.summary: Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexico was not aware of US participation until four officials were killed in car crash Claudia Sheinbaum , Mexico’s president, said on Monday that her government told the United States, in a diplomatic note, that the unauthorized presence of US officials at an anti-narcotics operation in the northern state of Chihuahua should not be repeated. The incident came to light after two US officials, along with two Mexican officials, were killed in a car crash on 19 April after th

stories.title: Indonesia: Many killed in train collision near Jakarta

stories.summary: A leading member of the Jalisco cartel and possible successor to "El Mencho," who was killed earlier this year, was nabbed by the Mexican military while hiding in a ditch.

stories.title: Canada's Arctic noise is no party for narwhals
stories.summary: Known as the "unicorns of the sea," narwhals are fleeing Canada's far north. Researchers suspect a link to noise pollution from increasing ship traffic.

stories.summary: Work has begun on a sprawling $15 billion AI hub in Visakhapatnam as India pushes to become a tech powerhouse. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Mali on edge as insurgency tests junta's resolve

stories.title: Iran beats Trump (at the World Snooker Championship)
stories.summary: Iran has defied the odds and scored a major victory over the world number one, Trump. It wasn’t in the Strait of Hormuz, however, but on the snooker table. In a shock result, Iran’s Hossein Vafaei defeated England’s Judd Trump at the World Snooker Championship to advance to the quarterfinals. Vafaei, who had to go through several qualifying rounds to make it to the tournament, beat Trump, the world’s top-ranked player, by 13 frames to 12. “I’m not scared of an

stories.title: Pro-choice campaigners in Malta create lockboxes containing abortion pills
stories.summary: Critics hit out at ‘dire’ situation in the country which has the strictest laws around abortion in western Europe Rights campaigners have affixed lockboxes containing abortion pills to sites across Malta, in a campaign designed to highlight the country’s near-total ban on abortion. The 15 black boxes aim to provide practical help to women grappling with the EU’s strictest abortion laws; anyone who is less than nine weeks pregnant and in need of an abortion is invited to send an email to obtain t

stories.title: EU faces ‘China shock’ as EV imports drive Beijing’s record surplus with bloc
stories.summary: China sold goods worth about $148bn to EU in first quarter of year, but imported just $65bn The EU is experiencing a prolonged “China shock” as a flood of Chinese EVs into Europe helped push Beijing to a record surplus with the bloc. New data showed China’s trade surplus – where its exports to the EU exceeded imports from the bloc – was $83bn (£61bn) in the first three months of 2026. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pro-Palestine activists appear in court over attack on Israeli arms factory in Germany
stories.summary: Families say ‘Ulm 5’ have been detained under extreme prison conditions since arrest last September Five pro-Palestinian activists have appeared in court over an attack on an Israeli arms company in Germany, charged with causing approximately €1m of damage. Prosecutors say the defendants, aged 25 to 40, trespassed and yelled pro-Palestinian statements as they destroyed office equipment, sensitive measuring devices and smashed windows at a site linked to Elbit Systems in the southern city of Ulm.

stories.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves 21 dead amid pre-election violence
stories.summary: Cocaine-trafficking rebels blamed for worst attack on civilians in decades, which also left 56 people injured The death toll in a Colombian highway bombing blamed on cocaine-trafficking rebels has risen to 21, the government said on Monday, in the country’s worst attack on civilians in decades and just ahead of elections. The attack on Saturday left 56 injured and buses and vans mangled on the Pan-American Highway, in the restive south-western Cauca department. Continue reading...

stories.title: German tourist dies after being bitten at snake show on family holiday in Egypt
stories.summary: Man, 57, was watching snake-charming show when reptile crawled into his trousers, say German police A German tourist has died after a snake crawled into his trousers and bit him as he watched a show in Egypt on a family holiday, police in Germany have said. The 57-year-old man was watching the snake-charming show at a hotel in Hurghada, a popular beach holiday destination on the Red Sea, in early April. Continue reading...

stories.title: US is being ‘humiliated’ by Iran’s leadership, says Friedrich Merz

Two days ago Donald Trump cancelled a trip by US negotiators to Islamabad for indirect talks with an Iranian delegation. A previous round in the Pakistani capital two weeks earlier, when JD Vance, the Amer

stories.title: Portugal's arms industry is taking off
stories.summary: Portugal has built up a surprisingly large, independent arms industry over the past five years. But whether it can be truly autonomous with the US as a major ally remains to be seen.

stories.title: Musk vs. OpenAI trial gets underway

stories.title: Will the Iran war end Strait of Hormuz oil supremacy?

This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-Presse. stories.title: Deadly Russian strikes hit Dnipro – video

stories.summary: Trump először ment elnökként az évente megrendezett eseményre, ahol a fél kormány és az amerikai sajtó színe-java megjelent, de nem tudta elmondani a beszédét, mert egy férfi fegyverrel a kezében a terem felé futott és lövések dördültek. Az igazságügyi miniszter szerint a hatóságok nem vallottak kudarcot, a beszámolók és a helyszínen készült videókon látható jelenetek többek szerint ennek ellent mondani látszanak.

stories.title: Queer as folk: The gay scandal that shook the German Empire
stories.summary: Germany's last emperor, a gilded porcelain vase and a general who had a heart attack in a pink tutu: The Eulenburg Affair shook the German Empire and Europe to its core and paved the way for the downfall of the monarchy.

stories.title: Sawe smashes 2-hour mark setting record at London Marathon
stories.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history in London as the first man to run a marathon in under two hours. He beat the world record by more than a minute.

stories.title: Germany pensions will fall short of living standards: Merz
stories.summary: Germany's state pension will only give "basic coverage" in retirement, Chancellor Friedrich Merz has warned amid hot debate. How does the system compare globally?

stories.title: SIPRI: Record arms spending, again
stories.summary: Global military spending hit a record high in 2025. The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute, or SIPRI, warns the trend is likely to continue — and carries additional risks.

stories.title: India news: Delhi to get respite, heat wave persists in other parts
stories.summary: Storms are expected in the capital, but severe heat across multiple states would continue, India's weather department said. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Germany news: Rise in young people refusing military service
stories.summary: Amid talk of a possible return of compulsory military service, more young Germans are applying for conscientious objector status. Meanwhile, Germany has become the world's fourth-largest military spender.

stories.title: World leaders react to White House press dinner shooting: no place for violence in democracy
stories.summary: Beyond condemning the attack, many leaders praised the US secret service, police and responders in ensuring the safety of guests. One officer was shot but was protected by a bulletproof vest and is in 'good condition', Trump said.

stories.title: On 40th Chernobyl disaster anniversary, Zelenskyy accuses Russia of committing 'nuclear terrorism'
stories.summary: Ukrainians gathered to honour those who died from the accident, as well as the hundreds of thousands who cleaned up after the disaster, exposing themselves to high levels of radiation.

stories.title: El-Sisi warns Middle East faces ‘attempts to redraw map’, calls for Gulf role in any Iran deal
stories.summary: The Egyptian president warned that the Middle East is entering a “critical phase”, with attempts to redraw its map, and called for state sovereignty to be respected.

stories.title: Iranian foreign minister heads to Moscow for meeting with Putin after second Pakistan trip
stories.summary: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi travelled once to Oman and twice to Pakistan over the weekend, before heading to Moscow to meet with Russian President Vladimir Putin on Monday.

stories.title: Did Trump save eight Iranian women from execution?
stories.summary: The US president says he saved eight Iranian women from execution, but Iran says otherwise.

stories.title: New IRA suspected in car bomb blast outside Northern Ireland police station
stories.summary: Police probe a car bomb outside Dunmurry station near Belfast, with two minors evacuated and the New IRA suspected of the overnight attack.

stories.title: Remembering Raghu Rai: The photographer who showed India to itself
stories.summary: With Rai's passing, India has lost a visual historian who was a chronicler of its most defining and often painful moments.

stories.title: Ukrainians mark 40th anniversary of Chernobyl disaster
stories.summary: Ukrainians in Slavutych on Sunday honoured the men and women whose mission it was to clean up the worst nuclear accident in history. People streamed in from different parts of the city to place candles on a large radiation hazard symbol as they marked the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster

stories.title: Trump evacuated after shots fired near White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Following the incident, US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the alleged suspect who reportedly opened fire near the ballroom where the annual correspondents' dinner was being held.

stories.title: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner
stories.summary: Security footage posted by President Donald Trump on social media appears to show the shooting suspect running toward White House Correspondents' dinner. Moments later, officials evacuated the area as gunshots were reported outside the ballroom.

stories.title: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfires As wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods. As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition : anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s yearly salary. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump cancels his envoys’ Pakistan trip for Iran ceasefire negotiations
stories.summary: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were to travel to Islamabad to attempt to revive ceasefire negotiations Donald Trump said he has told US envoys not to go to Pakistan for more talks with Iran , shortly after Tehran’s top diplomat left Islamabad late on Saturday. Trump added to Fox News: “They can call us anytime they want.” The White House on Friday said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would travel to Pakistan’s capital to attempt to revive ceasefire negotiations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Zelenskyy ready to hold Ukraine-Russia talks in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country is ready to hold talks with Russia in Azerbaijan. Meanwhile, Russian attacks killed several people across Ukraine.

stories.title: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today
stories.summary: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

stories.title: Trump cancels US negotiators' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks as Araghchi departs Islamabad
stories.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were expected to arrive in Pakistan on Saturday to conduct a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran.

stories.title: West Bank and part of Gaza vote in first municipal elections since war
stories.summary: Municipal elections in the West Bank and central Gaza mark the first vote since the Gaza war, with Fatah-linked lists dominant and Hamas absent from the race.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says he is ready to meet Putin in Azerbaijan
stories.summary: In a significant first visit to the South Caucasus since the start of Russia’s all-out war, Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in a statement next to President Ilham Aliyev he is ready to meet Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan after the US-led diplomatic talks have stalled over the past weeks.

stories.title: Russian strikes kill at least seven and injure dozens across Ukraine
stories.summary: Most of those killed and injured in Russian overnight attacks were in the eastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, where an attack killed at least five and injured more than 30.

stories.title: Palestinians vote in West Bank and central Gaza municipal elections, the first since war started
stories.summary: The elections, held in the Israeli-occupied West Bank and in Gaza's Deir al-Balah, are taking place against the backdrop of a highly volatile political and security environment.

stories.title: Watch: How one orangutan braved new bridge to unite his split community
stories.summary: The forest where the Sumatran orangutans live has been split by a road.

stories.title: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.title: Video shows correspondents' dinner suspect charge checkpoint
stories.summary: Surveillance video released by Donald Trump shows the suspect running past metal detectors as security agents draw their guns.

stories.title: Moment Trump rushed to safety as gunshots heard at White House Correspondents' Dinner
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump was quickly escorted to safety from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after gunshots were heard at the venue.

stories.title: Take action over officials in Kyrgyzstan ‘helping Russia evade sanctions’, MPs and peers say
stories.summary: British foreign secretary told to impose new measures as ruble-pegged cryptocurrency A7A5 is supported in country More than 20 MPs and peers have called on the foreign secretary to take action against institutions and individuals in Kyrgyzstan allegedly facilitating large-scale Russian sanctions evasion. They urged the UK to levy personal sanctions against three top Kyrgyz officials for their alleged role in facilitating Russian sanctions evasion more broadly, and more specifically for allowing

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian strikes in Dnipro, blackouts in Karachi and Manchester City’s Erling Haaland – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
stories.summary: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

stories.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
stories.summary: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

stories.title: Why investment in Caspian transit routes is crucial for energy security
stories.summary: As old energy routes buckle under the pressure of war and geopolitical disruption, governments and companies are converging on the Caspian region as the corridor that could keep Eurasia's energy flowing.

stories.title: UK agriculture deal with EU will not remove all red tape, peers told
stories.summary: Lords told sales of Scottish shellfish among areas that may benefit – but agreement will not erase all paperwork Europe live – latest updates A new agriculture agreement with the EU will not wipe out all Brexit paperwork but might pave the way for sales of Scottish langoustines and oysters, the House of Lords has heard. The UK and EU are close to finalising a sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) agreement to reduce Brexit trade barriers, and while it will have “modest” impact on the UK economy the a
